what: BSM=16 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2 waves=2 cache=None for M<=32 K>1024 fused path — replace legacy 32x64x512 config on the two M=32 large-K leaderboard shapes
why: Current M<=32 K>1024 path uses a single-row tile family with no software pipelining. A 16x128x256 retile keeps 2 M-tiles per M=32 problem, doubles N tile width, and restores NS=2 double-buffering, which may cut latency on the 32x2880x512? no, specifically 32x2880/4096 large-K leaderboard cases.

what: BSK=128 NW=4 NS=2 for M=64 fused path (from BSK=256) — same 16x128 tile, more but lighter K-iterations for the 64x7168x2048 leaderboard shape
why: The M=64 fused path currently does 8 iterations with 256-K chunks. Halving BSK reduces per-iteration register footprint and B/scale traffic, which may improve overlap and latency hiding without changing the proven 16x128 tile family.

what: waves_per_eu=1 for M<=32 K<=1024 fused path (from 2) — intermediate occupancy hint on the v211 cache=None configuration
why: The v211 improvement came from this exact path. waves_per_eu=2 may be slightly over-constraining register allocation for the two M=32 K=512 leaderboard shapes; waves_per_eu=1 is the only untested point between auto and the current explicit hint.
